title: Litigators of the Week: A Defense Verdict for Lockheed in Case Linking Birth Defects to Chemical Exposure | News | Greenberg Traurig LLP
content:
David B. Weinstein, Christopher Torres, Ryan Hopper, and Irina Khasin are featured in an Am Law Litigation Daily article titled “Litigators of the Week: A Defense Verdict for Lockheed in Case Linking Birth Defects to Chemical Exposure.”
After a five-week federal trial in Orlando, the team delivered a decisive victory for Lockheed Martin, proving that the company’s operations caused no harm to the children of three families who claimed otherwise. Despite Plaintiffs’ counsel asking the jury to award $790 million in damages, the nine-person jury deliberated for just over two hours before returning a complete defense verdict of $0 – a resounding affirmation of Lockheed Martin's commitment to the safety of its operations, employees, and the surrounding community.
Read “ Litigators of the Week: A Defense Verdict for Lockheed in Case Linking Birth Defects to Chemical Exposure ” on the Am Law Litigation Daily website. (subscription)
To download a PDF, click the media link below.
